Class Response

java.lang.Object
io.github.sentinel.apis.Response
public class Response extends Object
Wrapper for an http response for testing the response.
Author:
Sentinel Framework

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    dump()
    Returns a formatted string containing the response status, all headers, and the body.
    String
    extract(String jsonPath)
    Extracts a value from the JSON response using a JSONPath expression.
    List<String>
    extractList(String jsonPath)
    Extracts a list of values from the JSON response using a JSONPath expression.
    Map<String,String>
    getAllHeaders()
    Returns all response headers as a Map of name to value.
    String
    getContentType()
    Returns the Content-Type response header value, or null if not present.
    String
    getCookie(String name)
    Returns the value of the named cookie from the Set-Cookie response headers, or null if not present.
    Map<String,String>
    getCookies()
    Returns all cookies from Set-Cookie response headers as a Map of name to value.
    String
    getHeader(String name)
    Returns the value of the named response header, or null if not present.
    String
    getResponse()
    Returns the http response as a String
    int
    getResponseCode()
    Returns the response code from the response
    Duration
    getResponseTime()
    Returns the amount of time the response took to get.
    boolean
    isClientError()
    Returns true if the response code is in the 4xx client error range.
    boolean
    isServerError()
    Returns true if the response code is in the 5xx server error range.
    boolean
    isSuccessful()
    Returns true if the response code is in the 2xx success range.
    void
    setResponseTime(Duration duration)
    Sets the amount of time that the response took to get.
    List<String>
    validateSchema(String schemaFilePath)
    Validates the JSON response body against a JSON Schema file loaded from the classpath.

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Field Details

    • log

      private static final org.apache.logging.log4j.Logger log

    • httpResponse

      protected org.apache.http.HttpResponse httpResponse

    • jsonResponse

      protected String jsonResponse

    • responseTime

      protected Duration responseTime

  • Constructor Details

    • Response

      public Response(org.apache.http.HttpResponse httpResponse) throws IOException
      Parameters:
      httpResponse - HttpResponse the API call response used to create this object
      Throws:
      IOException - if the parsing fails

  • Method Details

    • getResponse

      public String getResponse()
      Returns the http response as a String
      Returns:
      String the http response

    • getResponseCode

      public int getResponseCode()
      Returns the response code from the response
      Returns:
      int the status of the response

    • setResponseTime

      public void setResponseTime(Duration duration)
      Sets the amount of time that the response took to get.
      Parameters:
      duration - Duration the response time

    • getResponseTime

      public Duration getResponseTime()
      Returns the amount of time the response took to get.
      Returns:
      Duration the response time

    • extract

      public String extract(String jsonPath)
      Extracts a value from the JSON response using a JSONPath expression.
      Parameters:
      jsonPath - String the JSONPath expression (e.g., "$.name")
      Returns:
      String the extracted value as a String, or null if not found

    • extractList

      public List<String> extractList(String jsonPath)
      Extracts a list of values from the JSON response using a JSONPath expression.
      Parameters:
      jsonPath - String the JSONPath expression targeting an array (e.g., "$.items[*].name")
      Returns:
      List<String> the extracted values, or an empty list if not found

    • getHeader

      public String getHeader(String name)
      Returns the value of the named response header, or null if not present.
      Parameters:
      name - String the header name (case-insensitive per HTTP spec)
      Returns:
      String the header value, or null

    • getAllHeaders

      public Map<String,String> getAllHeaders()
      Returns all response headers as a Map of name to value. If the same header name appears multiple times, the last value wins.
      Returns:
      Map<String, String> all response headers

    • getContentType

      public String getContentType()
      Returns the Content-Type response header value, or null if not present.
      Returns:
      String the Content-Type header value

    • getCookie

      public String getCookie(String name)
      Returns the value of the named cookie from the Set-Cookie response headers, or null if not present.
      Parameters:
      name - String the cookie name
      Returns:
      String the cookie value, or null

    • getCookies

      public Map<String,String> getCookies()
      Returns all cookies from Set-Cookie response headers as a Map of name to value.
      Returns:
      Map<String, String> cookie name-value pairs

    • dump

      public String dump()
      Returns a formatted string containing the response status, all headers, and the body.
      Returns:
      String the formatted response dump

    • validateSchema

      public List<String> validateSchema(String schemaFilePath)
      Validates the JSON response body against a JSON Schema file loaded from the classpath.
      Parameters:
      schemaFilePath - String the path relative to the classpath root (e.g., "schemas/pet.json")
      Returns:
      List<String> a list of validation error messages; empty if validation passes

    • isSuccessful

      public boolean isSuccessful()
      Returns true if the response code is in the 2xx success range.
      Returns:
      boolean true if successful

    • isClientError

      public boolean isClientError()
      Returns true if the response code is in the 4xx client error range.
      Returns:
      boolean true if client error

    • isServerError

      public boolean isServerError()
      Returns true if the response code is in the 5xx server error range.
      Returns:
      boolean true if server error
